findings.justification: Price separator settings (decimal/thousand) are sanitized with wp_kses_post, which allows HTML tags and attributes; a separator value containing allowed HTML (e.g. <a href>) is stored in the DB and later concatenated unescaped into price output HTML via number_format and wc_price, enabling stored XSS for admin-level users who can save WooCommerce settings.

The currency symbol returned by get_woocommerce_currency_symbol() is concatenated directly into the HTML return value without esc_html or esc_attr, so any hook or filter that modifies the currency symbol to include HTML/script content will inject it unescaped into rendered price markup.

When $regular_price or $sale_price are non-numeric strings, they are passed to wc_price() which returns HTML, but if already pre-formatted HTML is passed in directly, the result is embedded in <del>/<ins> tags without further escaping, allowing caller-controlled HTML injection.

The raw_woocommerce_price, formatted_woocommerce_price, and wc_price filters pass arbitrary plugin-modified values that are embedded directly into HTML output without escaping; a malicious or compromised plugin hooking these filters can inject arbitrary HTML into every rendered price on the site.

Non-numeric $from and $to values are passed directly into sprintf(_x(...)) and concatenated into the price HTML string without HTML-encoding; while wp_strip_all_tags is applied for screen-reader spans, the primary visible range display at line 1389 uses the raw non-numeric value unescaped.
